channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India narrowly defeats England in T20 World Cup semi-final, advances to face New Zealand
item.description: India ek zabardast semi-final match mein England ko sirf seven runs se hara diya aur T20 World Cup ke final mein New Zealand ka saamna karegi. Mumbai ke Wankhede stadium mein khele gaye match mein San...

item.title: Kolkata Emerges as Major IT Hub with Rapid Growth
item.description: Kolkata is witnessing a significant transformation in its IT landscape, particularly in areas like Sector V and New Town, which were once known for their serene water bodies and gardens. Today, these ...

item.title: Mail & Guardian publishes South African cryptic crossword JDE 520 by George Euvrard
item.description: The Mail & Guardian continues its tradition of offering a unique weekly cryptic crossword tailored specifically for South African readers. Compiled by George Euvrard, the JDE 520 edition, dated 5 Marc...

item.title: Australian Government Prohibits Hizb ut-Tahrir Under Strengthened Hate Group Legislation
item.description: The Albanese government has formally listed the radical Islamist group Hizb ut-Tahrir as a prohibited hate organisation, marking the first use of Australia's new hate group powers. This decision follo...

item.title: Alex Eala faces Dayana Yastremska in her Indian Wells Open debut
item.description: Philippine tennis sensation Alex Eala is set to start her first campaign in the prestigious WTA 1000 Indian Wells Open in California, United States. After receiving a first-round bye as one of the 32 ...

item.title: Ministry Reports Early Achievements in Women's Empowerment, Child Protection, and Elder Welfare
item.description: The Ministry of Community Development, Elders, Gender and Children in Zanzibar has shared its achievements from the first 100 days of President Hussein Mwinyi's second term. The Ministry focused on em...

item.title: Community Mourns Late Emurua Dikirr MP Johana Ng'eno and Others After Helicopter Crash
item.description: Hundreds of residents gathered at Emurua Dikirr Primary School to mourn the passing of their MP Johana Ng'eno and five other community members following a tragic helicopter crash in Nandi County. The ...

item.title: Nigeria Police Restructure: Some DIGs to Retire, Seven AIGs to Attend Promotion Interviews
item.description: Nigeria Police Force don set for major reshuffle as new Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Tunji Disu, don assume office. Sources confirm say some Deputy Inspectors-General (DIGs) go retire following ...

item.title: Kristi Noem Removed as Homeland Security Secretary Amid Controversial Policies
item.description: Kristi Noem has been removed from her role as Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security after a tenure marked by intense scrutiny over aggressive immigration enforcement and controversial feder...

item.title: Far North Queensland braces for more heavy rain and flash flooding as tropical low approaches coast
item.description: Far North Queensland is grappling with severe weather as a tropical low moves towards the coast, bringing the threat of heavy rain, flash flooding, and damaging winds. Although a cyclone watch for the...

item.description: As the UK online casino market continues to grow, several new operators have entered the scene, offering innovative features and competitive promotions. New casinos such as O’Reels, 7Bet, QuinnCasino,...

item.title: Rajya Sabha Elections: Bihar and Odisha Witness Key Contests Amid Opposition Alliances
item.description: The Rajya Sabha election nominations across ten states concluded on Thursday, highlighting critical tests for opposition political alliances against the NDA in Bihar and Odisha. In Bihar, the RJD has ...

item.title: Ontario Couple Faces High Costs After Discovery of Indigenous Ancestral Remains on Their Property
item.description: Christine and Dan Reio, a couple in Wainfleet, Ontario, discovered Indigenous ancestral remains on their property during a renovation project. The discovery has triggered a provincially mandated buria...

item.title: U.S. Stocks Rebound Amid Economic Data and Easing Oil Concerns
item.description: U.S. stock markets closed higher on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbing 238.14 points (0.49%) to 48,739.41, ending a three-day losing streak. The S&P 500 rose 0.78...

item.title: North Rift Artistes Gather in Eldoret to Honour MP Johana Ng'eno
item.description: North Rift artistes from across Uasin Gishu County and beyond gathered in Eldoret City to pay tribute to their patron, Embakasi Central MP Johana Ng'eno, through a vibrant event full of performances a...

item.title: Ponggay’s Escape Attempt Fails in the Latest Batang Quiapo Episode
item.description: In Episode 793 of 'Batang Quiapo,' Ponggay finds himself unable to escape from the clutches of the formidable Guerrero family. The episode showcases the intensity and suspense as Ponggay's plans are t...

item.title: Tanzanian Women Shine at Mining Indaba with Global Recognition
item.description: Tanzanian women are showing strong presence in the mining sector, gaining recognition both in Africa and globally. At the recent African Mining Indaba in South Africa, the African Women in Mining Asso...

item.title: UK Defence Secretary does not rule out participation in potential operations over Iran
item.description: UK Defence Secretary John Healey has avoided ruling out British involvement in offensive operations over Iran, during a visit to RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus. Speaking to Sky News, he stressed that military...

item.title: Chinese Embassy rejects allegations of Filipino espionage linked to China
item.description: The Chinese Embassy in Manila has officially denied any involvement of Beijing in alleged espionage activities involving Filipinos, following reports from the National Security Council (NSC). Spokespe...

item.title: Federal Government Approves Reconstruction of Carter Bridge in Lagos
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don approve say dem go demolish and rebuild Carter Bridge wey dey Lagos, one of di oldest bridges wey dey connect Lagos Island to di mainland. Di decision come after Federal Exec...
